narrative: PLACEHOLDER (subagent): gathered index closes/weekly returns and high-level drivers; parent agent to complete full briefing with picks/movers.

value_picks.name: Salesforce, Inc.
value_picks.why_undervalued: Shares were printing 52-week lows during the week ending 2026-02-06, reflecting investor worries about software demand and AI spend payback periods despite Salesforce’s scale and recurring revenue base.
value_picks.bull_case: If enterprise IT budgets stabilize and Salesforce sustains margin/FCF expansion from cost discipline, the stock can re-rate back toward prior cycle multiples and recover a meaningful portion of the drawdown.

value_picks.name: Automatic Data Processing, Inc.
value_picks.why_undervalued: ADP hit a 52-week low on 2026-02-06 despite its mission-critical payroll franchise and consistently positive cash generation, suggesting a sentiment-driven multiple reset more than a fundamental break.
value_picks.bull_case: As hiring trends normalize, ADP’s earnings durability and shareholder returns (dividends/buybacks) can drive a rebound toward its prior valuation range.

value_picks.name: Crown Castle Inc.
value_picks.why_undervalued: CCI was also at a 52-week low on 2026-02-06 amid rate sensitivity and tower REIT skepticism, even though the asset base is long-duration and cash generative.
value_picks.bull_case: Falling rates and renewed carrier spending could improve sentiment, allowing CCI’s dividend and cash flows to support a recovery.

value_picks.name: MGM Resorts International
value_picks.why_undervalued: MGM screened as undervalued in February 2026 cash-flow based screens despite generating meaningful free cash flow and owning premium Strip assets; the market appeared to be discounting cyclical travel/gaming risk.
value_picks.bull_case: If Las Vegas and digital/BetMGM trends hold up, MGM’s low cash-flow multiple provides room for upside via buybacks and multiple expansion.

value_picks.name: Veeva Systems Inc.
value_picks.why_undervalued: VEEV was near 52-week lows on 2026-02-06, indicating broad software risk-off despite its sticky life-sciences vertical franchise and strong cash generation.
value_picks.bull_case: Continuation of high retention and expansion into adjacent applications could restore growth confidence and support a rebound.

momentum_picks.name: Rackspace Technology, Inc.
momentum_picks.catalyst: RXT surged after its Q4/FY 2025 results and 2026 outlook signaled improving operating profit and cash generation, fueling a sharp short-covering/turnaround rally.
momentum_picks.risk_reward: High upside if the turnaround persists, but elevated downside given leverage and historically volatile execution.

momentum_picks.name: Fastly, Inc.
momentum_picks.catalyst: Fastly was among February 2026’s top monthly gainers, consistent with a risk-on rebound in higher-beta software/networking names.
momentum_picks.risk_reward: Momentum is strong but valuation and growth durability remain key risks if customer demand softens.

momentum_picks.name: Applied Optoelectronics, Inc.
momentum_picks.catalyst: AAOI ranked among the top February 2026 gainers, reflecting strong investor appetite for AI/data-center optical components.
momentum_picks.risk_reward: Positive if AI infra capex continues, but the stock is sensitive to cyclicality and customer concentration.

momentum_picks.name: Ichor Holdings, Ltd.
momentum_picks.catalyst: ICHR’s February 2026 surge fits the broader semicap equipment upcycle narrative tied to data-center buildouts.
momentum_picks.risk_reward: Upside with wafer-fab equipment strength, but downside if capex pauses or margins compress.

momentum_picks.name: Clear Secure, Inc.
momentum_picks.catalyst: YOU was a notable February 2026 gainer amid improving travel volumes and expanding identity platform adoption.
momentum_picks.risk_reward: Strong momentum, but any slowdown in airport enrollment growth could trigger a sharp reset.
